cards.title: Chest Pain — Differential Diagnosis
cards._legacyHtml:
Rule Out Life-Threatening First ★
Always exclude ACS, PE, aortic dissection, tension pneumothorax before considering benign causes. These 4 kill within minutes to hours.
DiagnosisCharacterKey FeaturesNaturopathic Role
ACS / MI ★Crushing, radiates jaw/arm, >20 minDiaphoresis, nausea, ECG changes, troponin ↑REFER EMERGENCY — no naturopathic Rx
Pulmonary embolism ★Pleuritic, sudden onset, worse breathingTachycardia, hypoxia, Wells score, D-dimer, CTPAREFER EMERGENCY
Aortic dissection ★Tearing, worst ever, radiates to backBP differential both arms, wide mediastinum CXRREFER EMERGENCY
GERD / oesophagealBurning, substernal, worse lying/bendingRelieved antacids, waterbrash, post-mealDiet, elevation, DGL, probiotics
MusculoskeletalSharp, localised, worse movement/palpationReproducible on pressing, follows injuryPhysical therapy, anti-inflammatory herbs
CostochondritisSharp, anterior chest, worse breathingTender on sternal/costal palpationNSAID herbs (willow, turmeric), rest
Anxiety/panicTight, squeezing, with palpitationsBreathlessness, fear, tingling, resolves 20 minBreathing techniques, adaptogens, CBT
PleurisyPleuritic, worse inspirationPleural rub, fever, follows respiratory illnessRest, anti-inflammatory — identify cause

cards.title: Abdominal Pain — Differential by Location
cards._legacyHtml:
LocationTop DifferentialsKey Features
RUQCholecystitis, biliary colic, hepatitis, Fitz-Hugh-CurtisMurphy's sign, fat-food trigger, jaundice
LUQSplenic pathology, pancreatitis, gastric ulcer, renalKehr's sign (diaphragmatic irritation → left shoulder pain)
RIFAppendicitis, ovarian cyst/torsion, ectopic, IBD (Crohn's), psoas abscessRovsing's, psoas, obturator signs
LIFDiverticulitis, sigmoid volvulus, IBD (UC), ovarian"Left-sided appendicitis" in elderly
EpigastricPUD, GERD, pancreatitis, ACS (BEWARE), gastritisAlways do ECG — MI can present as epigastric pain
PeriumbilicalEarly appendicitis (migrates RIF), SBO, mesenteric ischaemiaMigration to RIF = appendicitis until proven otherwise
SuprapubicUTI, cystitis, PID, fibroids, prostatitis, appendicitis (pelvic)MSSU, pelvic exam, pregnancy test (women)
GeneralisedPeritonitis, gastroenteritis, IBS, mesenteric adenitis, DKAPeritonism: rigidity, guarding, rebound — REFER
🟡 Red Flags — Refer Immediately
Rigid abdomen · Rebound tenderness · Haemodynamic instability · Signs of peritonism · Suspected ectopic pregnancy (women of reproductive age + abdominal pain + amenorrhoea = ectopic until proven otherwise) · Obstruction (vomiting + distension + no bowel sounds) · Any severe pain not improving in 2 hours.

cards.title: Headache — Differential Diagnosis
cards._legacyHtml:
Red Flag Headaches — SNOOP ★
Systemic symptoms (fever, weight loss) · Neurological signs · Onset sudden (thunderclap) · Older age (>50 new headache) · Prior history change (pattern change in known migraineur) → CT/LP mandatory before naturopathic treatment.
TypeLocationCharacterDurationNaturopathic Approach
Tension headacheBilateral, band-likePressure/tightness, mild-moderate30 min–7 daysMagnesium 400mg, peppermint oil topical, stress management, posture
Migraine (without aura)UnilateralPulsating, moderate-severe, nausea, photophobia4–72 hrsMagnesium 600mg, riboflavin 400mg, CoQ10, feverfew, avoid triggers
Migraine with auraUnilateralPreceded by visual/sensory aura 20–60 min4–72 hrsSame + omega-3, identify food triggers (tyramine, alcohol, chocolate)
Cluster headacheUnilateral orbital/periorbitalExcruciating, boring, autonomic features (watering eye)15–180 min, clusters over weeksMelatonin 10mg at night, refer for oxygen therapy — very limited naturopathic Rx
CervicogenicOccipital, radiates frontalDull, worsened by neck movementHours–daysPhysiotherapy, chiropractic, hydrotherapy, posture correction
Subarachnoid ★Global, "thunderclap""Worst headache of life" sudden onsetConstant → EMERGENCYREFER EMERGENCY — do not treat

cards.title: Fatigue — Differential Diagnosis
cards._legacyHtml:
CategoryCauseKey FeaturesTestsNaturopathic Focus
EndocrineHypothyroidismCold intolerance, weight gain, constipation, bradycardiaTSH, free T4Selenium, iodine, ashwagandha — support alongside medication
EndocrineAdrenal insufficiencyFatigue worse mornings, salt craving, postural hypotensionMorning cortisol, ACTH stimAdaptogens, B5, Vit C — refer for diagnosis first
HaematologicalIron deficiency anaemiaPallor, dyspnoea on exertion, pica, koilonychiaCBC, ferritin, serum ironIron-rich foods, Vit C with iron, remove inhibitors (tea/coffee with meals)
HaematologicalB12/folate deficiencyFatigue, glossitis, neuropathy, macrocytosisB12, folate, homocysteineMethylcobalamin (B12), methylfolate — correct deficiency
Mental healthDepressionLow mood, anhedonia, sleep change, cognitive slowingPHQ-9, TSH (exclude thyroid)Exercise, omega-3, St John's Wort (mild-moderate), CBT, sunlight
Sleep disorderOSANon-refreshing sleep, snoring, witnessed apnoeas, morning headacheEpworth, sleep studyWeight loss, lateral sleep position, CPAP — refer sleep clinic
InfectionPost-viral (COVID, EBV)Follows acute infection, PEM (exertion worsens)EBV serology, inflammatory markersPacing, rest, Vit C, zinc, adaptogens — AVOID vigorous exercise (worsens PEM)
MetabolicT2DM / prediabetesPolyuria, polydipsia, blurred vision, recurrent infectionsFBG, HbA1c, OGTTLow GI diet, berberine, exercise, weight loss

cards.title: Skin Rash — Diagnostic Approach
cards._legacyHtml:
1
Describe the lesion (SCAM) S — Size/Shape. C — Colour. A — Arrangement (grouped/linear/dermatomal/scattered). M — Morphology: macule (flat) → papule (raised <5mm) → plaque (raised >5mm) → vesicle (fluid <5mm) → bulla (>5mm) → pustule (pus) → wheal (transient/urticaria).
2
Key diagnostic questions Itch: present (eczema, urticaria, scabies, lichen planus) or absent (psoriasis, pityriasis). Distribution: sun-exposed (photosensitivity, SLE, rosacea), flexures (eczema), extensor (psoriasis), dermatomal (herpes zoster), palms and soles (secondary syphilis, scabies, HFMD). Systemic features: fever (viral exanthem, meningococcal, Kawasaki), arthralgia (SLE, reactive), weight loss (malignancy).
PatternDistributionKey FeatureDiagnosis
Silver scaly plaquesExtensor surfaces, scalp, nailsAuspitz sign, nail pittingPsoriasis
Erythematous, weeping, pruriticFlexures (cubital, popliteal)Personal/family atopyAtopic eczema
Vesicles — dermatomal ★Single dermatome, unilateralPain precedes rash 2–3 daysHerpes zoster (shingles)
Butterfly malar rashCheeks, spares nasolabial foldPhotosensitive, systemic featuresSLE
Transient whealsAnywhere, migrating, <24hrsIntense itchUrticaria
Target (iris) lesionsExtremities, trunkConcentric ringsErythema multiforme
Petechiae/purpura ★Non-blanching (glass test)Fever = EMERGENCYVasculitis, meningococcal, ITP
Annular scaly ringTrunk, limbsClear centre, advancing edgeTinea (ringworm)
Burrows — interdigitalWeb spaces, wrists, waistNocturnal itch, household contactsScabies

cards.title: Weight Gain, Oedema & Back Pain — Differentials
cards._legacyHtml:
UNEXPLAINED WEIGHT GAIN
CauseKey FeaturesTests
Hypothyroidism ★Cold intolerance, fatigue, constipation, dry skin, bradycardiaTSH ★, free T4
PCOSIrregular periods, hirsutism, acne, central obesity, acanthosis nigricansLH, FSH, testosterone, USS pelvis, fasting insulin
Cushing's syndromeCentral obesity, moon face, buffalo hump, purple striae, proximal myopathy, HTN24hr urinary cortisol, overnight dexamethasone suppression test
Insulin resistanceCentral obesity, acanthosis nigricans, fatigue after carbs, FHx T2DMHOMA-IR, fasting glucose, HbA1c
Drug-induced ★Antipsychotics (olanzapine), antidepressants (mirtazapine), steroids, insulinMedication timeline vs weight gain onset
Rapid fluid retention>2kg/week, ankle oedema, breathlessnessBNP, renal function, urinalysis — refer urgently
MONOARTHRITIS vs POLYARTHRITIS
DiagnosisJointsKey FeaturesTest
Septic arthritis ★Mono (knee 50%)Hot, swollen, fever, cannot weight-bearASPIRATE FIRST + blood cultures
Gout1st MTP ★, ankle, kneeExcruciating overnight onset, tophi (chronic)Aspirate: needle crystals, negatively birefringent
PseudogoutKnee ★, wrist (elderly)X-ray: chondrocalcinosis ★Aspirate: rhomboid crystals, positively birefringent
Rheumatoid arthritisMCPs, PIPs, wrists — symmetricMorning stiffness >1hr, spares DIPsRF, anti-CCP (most specific)
Psoriatic arthritisDIP, dactylitis (sausage digit ★), asymmetricPsoriasis (check scalp, nails, umbilicus)No specific test — X-ray advanced
